summary: Joel Spolsky posts a job listing seeking a technical writer to update the FogBugz project management book for version 6.0. The original author Mike Gunderloy is unavailable, and the revision requires about 40% new content plus refreshed screenshots due to a major UI overhaul. Joel highlights the book's value as both documentation and a software project management introduction. An update notes that Gunderloy ultimately found time to do the revision himself.
key_points: FogBugz 6.0 introduced enough new features and UI changes to require a substantial book revision with 40% more content
The book served a dual purpose as both product documentation and a general introduction to software project management
The update revealing Mike Gunderloy's return suggests a preference for continuity of authorship over bringing in someone new
money_quotes: More than just a manual, this book is a great introduction to software project management.
I think we'll all be better off if this book will become the standard against which other end-user documentation will be measured.
key_insight: Good product documentation should transcend mere feature descriptions and serve as a genuine introduction to the discipline the product supports.
